definitions.text: The portion of the upper human appendage, from the shoulder to the wrist and sometimes including the hand.
examples: She stood with her right arm extended and her palm forward to indicate “Stop!”

definitions.text: To take by the arm; to take up in one's arms.
Poor; lacking in riches or wealth.

examples: The Duke's arms were a sable gryphon rampant on an argent field.

definitions.text: War; hostilities; deeds or exploits of war. (Can we add an example for this sense?)
To supply with armour or (later especially) weapons.
examples: The king armed his knights with swords and shields.

definitions.text: To supply with the equipment, knowledge, authority, or other tools needed for a particular task; to furnish with capability; to equip.
To prepare (a tool, weapon, or system) for action; to activate.
examples: Remember to arm the alarm system before leaving for work.
